steps: Queried antimicrobial peptide database. Training source: modlAMP (2600 AMPs + 2600 non-AMPs). Found 19 AMPs with relevant activity against Fusarium graminearum. Selected top 10 as seeds for optimization.

Generated 150 variants from 10 seed peptides. Strategies: 35 point mutation, 53 charge optimization, 25 n terminal truncation, 25 c terminal truncation, 12 scrambled control. Substitutions prioritize cationic residues (+K/R) to increase charge and hydrophobic residues (+L/W/F) to improve amphipathicity. Included scrambled controls to validate that sequence order matters.

Generated vector representations of 160 peptides using ESM-2 (650M params), a protein language model trained on 65 million sequences (UniRef50). Each peptide is a 1280 dimensional vector capturing evolutionary and structural properties. Ofvice: CUDA.

Computed 12 biochemical properties for 160 candidates. Effective AMPs have charge +2 to +9 and high hydrophobic moment. Of 160 candidates: 142 with ideal charge, 116 with high amphipathicity. Average charge: +5.2.

Trained classifier with AUC 0.977 ± 0.004 in 5-fold cross-validation. Training data: 2600 positive AMPs, 2600 negatives. Most predictive features: ESM2_PC3 (0.119), aa_M (0.077), ESM2_PC2 (0.074), net_charge_ph7 (0.036), isoelectric_point (0.034) — consistent with literature identifying net charge and hydrophobic moment as key AMP properties. Classified 160 candidates.

Toxicity screening completed for 160 candidates. 159 passed all filters (risk < 0.4). 1 flagged for elevated risk. Most common alerts: Cysteine-rich (22% > 12%) (23x); Contains motif WxxW/WxxxW (21x); High charge (+8.0) (12x). Los candidates aprobados tienen perfil favorable de selectividad patógeno/host.
